latHint: Think Aesop and a fable's one-line takeaway.
pivHint: GOOD in this psalm describes the Lord's character. But every fable has a lesson at the end too - a "GOOD" lesson about right and wrong. What do we call that kind of lesson?
winText: You found it. The Lord is GOOD, and every story's lesson about right and wrong is called its MORAL - goodness has a way of teaching itself.
lossText: The word was MORAL. GOOD was the pivot - the Lord's unchanging character in the psalm, and the lesson every GOOD story leaves behind.
votdVerse: For the LORD is good and his love endures forever; his faithfulness continues through all generations.
passageText: Shout for joy to the LORD, all the earth. Worship the LORD with gladness; come before him with joyful songs. Know that the LORD is God. It is he who made us, and we are his; we are his people, the sheep of his pasture. Enter his gates with thanksgiving and his courts with praise; give thanks to him and praise his name. For the LORD is good and his love endures forever; his faithfulness continues through all generations.
